title: Early Stopping: Knowing When to Stop Training
concept: Early stopping is a regularization technique that monitors validation loss during training and stops the process automatically when the validation score stops improving, preventing overfitting.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is indicated when the validation loss curve begins to bend upward while training loss continues to fall?
exercises.explain: Correct! Rising validation loss paired with falling training loss is the hallmark of overfitting.

cards.title: Taking cookies out of the oven
cards.body: If you bake cookies, you don't just set a random timer for 5 hours. You look through the oven window. The moment they turn golden brown, you take them out. If you leave them in longer, they burn. Early stopping is this oven window for training neural networks.

cards.title: Early Stopping: The automatic exit
cards.body: Early stopping is a regularization method. During training, the program monitors the validation loss after every epoch. If the validation loss stops dropping and starts rising for a set number of epochs (called **patience**), the program halts training automatically and saves the best version.

cards.title: The tired student
cards.body: Imagine studying for an exam. In the first 2 hours, you learn a lot. By hour 4, you are exhausted, and you start misinterpreting simple facts, actually getting worse. A tutor (early stopping) notices your score dropping, says 'Stop studying, go sleep,' and locks in your peak hour-2 knowledge.

cards.title: Tuning patience
cards.body: We configure early stopping with `patience = 5`. The validation loss drops until epoch 15 (value = 0.35). In epochs 16-20, the loss rises slightly `[0.36, 0.38, 0.37, 0.39, 0.40]`. Since it failed to improve for 5 consecutive steps, the training halts at epoch 20, and the epoch-15 weights are restored.

cards.title: Stopping early doesn't mean incomplete training
cards.body: You might think stopping training early means the model is 'unfinished.' In reality, running more epochs just allows the model to over-optimize on training noise. Stopping at the validation valley is when the model is mathematically complete for the real world; more epochs only degrade performance.

cards.title: When Early Stopping Triggers
cards.body: Finding the sweet spot on the learning curve:

left.text:
Validation loss dropping.
Keep training.
Model is learning patterns.

right.text:
Validation loss starts climbing.
Patience window runs out.
Halt training, restore best weights.

exercises.prompt: How does early stopping prevent a neural network from overfitting?

options.text: It monitors validation loss and automatically halts training when the validation score stops improving, preserving the best weights.
It deletes the inputs that are causing the errors.
It forces the learning rate to double.
It converts the model's layers from non-linear to linear.

exercises.explain: Correct! Early stopping halts training when validation loss stops dropping, locking in the best model.

exercises.prompt: True or false: The 'patience' setting in early stopping determines how many epochs the algorithm will wait without validation improvement before halting.
exercises.explain: True! Patience is the buffer window allowed to ensure the loss is truly rising and not just experiencing a temporary bounce.

exercises.sentence: Early stopping is classified as a ___ technique because it prevents overfitting and helps the model generalize.
exercises.explain: Early stopping is a classic regularization method based on stopping training at the optimal validation moment.

exercises.prompt: A model has patience = 3. Validation loss over epochs 10-15: [0.4, 0.3, 0.32, 0.34, 0.35, 0.36]. At which epoch does early stopping halt the training?

options.text: Epoch 14, because loss failed to improve for 3 steps after the epoch-11 valley (0.3).
Epoch 15, because that was the final logged epoch.
Epoch 10, because that was the starting point.

exercises.explain: Correct! The lowest point was at epoch 11 (0.3). The loss rose at epoch 12, 13, and 14 (3 consecutive misses). The trigger fires at epoch 14, restoring the epoch-11 weights.

exercises.prompt: You set up early stopping with patience = 10. The training runs for 150 epochs before triggering. The validation loss valley was at epoch 45 (value = 0.12). What is the state of the final model weights saved to disk?

options.text: The algorithm restores and saves the weights from epoch 45, which achieved the lowest validation loss.
The weights are from epoch 150, because the last epoch is always saved.
The weights are reset to random initialization values.
The weights are from epoch 55 due to the patience offset.

exercises.explain: Exactly! When early stopping halts, it restores the weights from the absolute best epoch recorded during the run (epoch 45), discarding the subsequent overfitted weights. Congratulations on completing the Training Loop unit! Next, let's bring everything together in a hands-on forward pass capstone!
